---
format:
  dashboard:
    embed-resources: false
    theme:
      - default
      - lib/custom.scss
    nav-buttons: []
    orientation: rows
params:
  all_data_path: ""
  cohort_colors_json: ""
  period_index_json: ""
  render_ts: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Anchor knitr to project root (Quarto otherwise runs chunks from templates/).
proj_root <- normalizePath("..", winslash = "/")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = proj_root)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(plotly); library(DT); library(htmltools)
  library(jsonlite)
})
for (f in c("utils.R", "data_layer.R", "portfolio_runner.R",
            "comparison_engine.R", "ai_interpret.R",
            "plot_helpers.R", "html_components.R")) {
  source(file.path(proj_root, "R", f), local = FALSE)
}

# ---- payload read ---------------------------------------------------------
all_data <- readRDS(params$all_data_path)
all_data <- Filter(function(r) !is.null(r) && length(r$periods) > 0, all_data)
stopifnot(length(all_data) >= 1)

# cohort_colors as a named character vector (source of truth, parsed from
# render_dashboard.R's computed assignment — do NOT recompute here).
cohort_colors <- jsonlite::fromJSON(params$cohort_colors_json, simplifyVector = TRUE)
if (!is.character(cohort_colors) || length(cohort_colors) == 0) {
  # Defensive fallback: compute from labels if caller forgot to pass.
  cohort_colors <- assign_cohort_colors(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1)))
}

period_index_json <- params$period_index_json
cohort_colors_json <- params$cohort_colors_json

primary   <- all_data[[length(all_data)]]
render_ts <- if (nzchar(params$render_ts %||% "")) {
  params$render_ts
} else {
  format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
}

# Ensure all_data is keyed by cohort_label so per-cohort lookups stay clean.
names(all_data) <- vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1))

# ---- helpers scoped to this template --------------------------------------
# cohort_color_for: safe lookup that falls through to navy if missing.
cohort_color_for <- function(cohort_label) {
  col <- cohort_colors[[cohort_label]]
  if (is.null(col) || is.na(col) || !nzchar(col)) CVM_PALETTE$navy else col
}
# Raw-HTML fence helper — same pattern as other templates; required so
# pandoc does not re-parse inline <span> tags as markdown text.
emit_html <- function(tag) {
  if (is.null(tag)) return(invisible())
  cat("\n```{=html}\n", as.character(tag), "\n```\n\n", sep = "")
}

# ---- overview scalars ------------------------------------------------------
overall_medians <- vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$overall_median %||% NA_real_, numeric(1))
total_students  <- sum(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$total_students  %||% 0L, integer(1)))
total_periods   <- sum(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0L, integer(1)))
n_tag_sets_max  <- max(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$n_tag_sets %||% 0L, integer(1)), 0L)

# ---- per-cohort precomputed frames (used by Assessment Performance tab) ----
cohort_trends <- lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok)) return(NULL)
  median_trend(r$grades_df)
})

# cohort_thresholds: count of students with <70%, <80%, <90% final grade.
# Shape: tibble(threshold = <chr>, count = <int>)
thresholds_from_gb <- function(gb) {
  final <- gb |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                          "individual_weighted_overall"))
  if (nrow(final) == 0) final <- gb
  g <- final$grade
  tibble::tibble(
    threshold = c("< 70%", "< 80%", "< 90%"),
    count     = c(sum(g < 70, na.rm = TRUE),
                  sum(g < 80, na.rm = TRUE),
                  sum(g < 90, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
}
cohort_thresholds <- lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) return(NULL)
  thresholds_from_gb(r$grades_df)
})

# period_labels: "34" -> "GC26 · Fall Y1". Drives readable x-axis ticks on the
# trend chart. Built from build_period_index() which already does this work.
pi <- build_period_index(all_data)
period_labels <- stats::setNames(
  vapply(pi, function(p) as.character(p$label), character(1)),
  vapply(pi, function(p) as.character(p$cperiod_id), character(1))
)

# cohort_stats: per-cohort summary rows below the trend chart.
cohort_stats <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Cohort = lab, Median = NA_real_, Mean = NA_real_,
      SD = NA_real_, Min = NA_real_, Max = NA_real_,
      N = 0L, `% ≥ 90` = NA_real_, `% ≥ 80` = NA_real_,
      .rows = 1
    ))
  }
  # Use the final-grade assessments where available; fall back to all rows.
  final <- r$grades_df |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                          "individual_weighted_overall"))
  df <- if (nrow(final) > 0) final else r$grades_df
  g <- df$grade[!is.na(df$grade)]
  tibble::tibble(
    Cohort   = lab,
    Median   = stats::median(g),
    Mean     = mean(g),
    SD       = stats::sd(g),
    Min      = min(g),
    Max      = max(g),
    N        = dplyr::n_distinct(df$id),
    `% ≥ 90` = 100 * mean(g >= 90),
    `% ≥ 80` = 100 * mean(g >= 80)
  )
}))

# cohorts_with_data: keep the order of names(all_data) but drop any that have
# no gradebook — used by the histogram grid below so we don't render empty
# panels.
cohorts_with_data <- Filter(function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) && !is.null(r$grades_df) && nrow(r$grades_df) > 0
}, names(all_data))

# cohort_breakdown_df: single long table for the breakdown DT — one row per
# assessment × cohort × period.
cohort_breakdown_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
    r <- all_data[[lab]]
    if (is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) return(NULL)
    bd <- assessment_breakdown(r$grades_df)
    if (nrow(bd) == 0) return(NULL)
    bd$cohort <- lab
    bd$cohort_color <- unname(cohort_colors[[lab]])
    bd
  })
)

# Tag-sets available are derived from the primary (latest) cohort's coverage.
tag_set_names <- if (!is.null(primary$tag_coverage) && length(primary$tag_coverage) > 0) {
  names(primary$tag_coverage[[1]] %||% list())
} else character(0)

# Chip list order for sidebar display (pretty names).
tag_set_pretty <- if (length(tag_set_names) > 0) pretty_tagset_name(tag_set_names) else character(0)
```

---
title: "`r sprintf('%s — %s (All years)', primary$course_code, primary$course_name)`"
---

# {.sidebar}

```{r}
ui_sidebar(
  course_code = primary$course_code,
  course_name = primary$course_name,
  metadata = c(
    sprintf("All-years view — %d cohort%s",
            length(all_data),
            if (length(all_data) == 1) "" else "s"),
    sprintf("%d periods total", total_periods),
    sprintf("Rendered %s", render_ts)
  ),
  extra_sections = list(
    list(title = "Classes included",
         items = vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1))),
    list(title = "Tag sets available",
         items = tag_set_pretty)
  )
)
```

# Overview

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Portfolio at a glance"
# KPI cards carry data-kpi hooks so the filter JS can recompute on chip
# toggle. Initial values reflect ALL cohorts selected.
tagList(
  ui_kpi_row(
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Pooled median grade (%)",
      value    = if (any(!is.na(overall_medians))) sprintf("%.1f", median(overall_medians, na.rm = TRUE)) else "—",
      subvalue = "Across selected cohorts",
      kpi_key  = "median"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Total students assessed",
      value    = fmt_num(total_students),
      subvalue = "Summed across selected cohorts",
      kpi_key  = "students"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Curriculum periods covered",
      value    = fmt_num(total_periods),
      kpi_key  = "periods"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Curriculum tag sets",
      value    = fmt_num(n_tag_sets_max),
      subvalue = "Distinct curriculum tag sets",
      kpi_key  = "tag_sets",
      size     = "sm"
    )
  )
)
```

```{r}
#| title: "Cohort summary"
# One row per cohort: colored dot + label + individual median / students.
rows <- lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
  # Pull per-cohort stats we already computed in the setup chunk — keeps a
  # single source of truth with the Assessment Performance stats card.
  stat <- cohort_stats[cohort_stats$Cohort == lab, , drop = FALSE]
  take_num <- function(col_name) {
    if (nrow(stat) == 0) return(NA_real_)
    v <- stat[[col_name]]
    if (length(v) == 0 || !is.finite(v[[1]])) NA_real_ else round(as.numeric(v[[1]]), 1)
  }
  data.frame(
    Cohort       = lab,
    Color        = col,
    Median       = take_num("Median"),
    Mean         = take_num("Mean"),
    SD           = take_num("SD"),
    Min          = take_num("Min"),
    Max          = take_num("Max"),
    `N Students` = as.integer(r$kpi$total_students  %||% 0L),
    `% ≥ 90`     = take_num("% ≥ 90"),
    `% ≥ 80`     = take_num("% ≥ 80"),
    Periods      = as.integer(r$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0L),
    `Tag Sets`   = as.integer(r$kpi$n_tag_sets       %||% 0L),
    check.names  = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})
summary_df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
# Cohort cell = colored dot + label. The containing <tr> is NOT wrapped in
# .cvm-filterable (DT rebuilds rows dynamically, and hiding rows via the
# filter JS would break DT's rendering). The chip filter bar drives the OTHER
# per-cohort cards and charts; this summary table intentionally always shows
# every cohort so the cross-cohort comparison stays visible.
summary_df$Cohort <- mapply(function(lab, col) {
  sprintf('<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;"></span>%s',
          col, htmlEscape(lab))
}, summary_df$Cohort, summary_df$Color, USE.NAMES = FALSE)
summary_df$Color <- NULL

# Spec-ordered columns: Cohort, Median, Mean, SD, Min, Max, N Students,
# % ≥ 90, % ≥ 80, Periods, Tag Sets.
summary_df <- summary_df[, c("Cohort", "Median", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max",
                             "N Students", "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80",
                             "Periods", "Tag Sets"), drop = FALSE]

numeric_cols <- c("Median", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max",
                  "N Students", "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80", "Periods", "Tag Sets")
numeric_col_idx0 <- which(names(summary_df) %in% numeric_cols) - 1L

DT::datatable(
  summary_df,
  rownames = FALSE,
  escape   = FALSE,
  options  = list(
    dom = "t", pageLength = 20, ordering = FALSE, scrollX = FALSE,
    columnDefs = list(
      list(targets = 0, className = "dt-left"),
      list(targets = numeric_col_idx0, className = "dt-right")
    ),
    initComplete = DT::JS(
      "function(settings, json) {",
      "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
      "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
      "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
      "    'font-weight':      '600',",
      "    'border-bottom':    '3px solid #AB8B00'",
      "  });",
      "}"
    )
  )
) |>
  # All numeric columns to 1 decimal (integer counts like N/Periods/Tag Sets
  # are stored as integers and display without decimals via DT's int path).
  DT::formatRound(columns = c("Median", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max",
                              "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80"), digits = 1)
```

# Assessment Performance

## Row

```{r}
# Assessment Performance — redesigned as five vertically stacked full-width
# sections. Each section has a navy 18px bold heading, a one-line muted
# description, 32px vertical padding, and is separated from the next by a
# 1px #E8ECF0 hr divider. The earlier chunk-isolation-per-widget
# requirement was only needed because of the `styleEqual(NULL, NULL)` /
# `onRender` bugs (both since removed), so all five widgets now live in a
# single tagList for a cleaner presentation flow.

section_head <- function(title, desc) {
  tagList(
    tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", title),
    tags$p(class  = "cvm-card-desc",  desc)
  )
}

# ── Section 1: Grade Performance by Cohort ──────────────────────────────
s1_widget <- if (nrow(cohort_stats) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook data available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  stats_df <- cohort_stats
  stats_df$Cohort <- vapply(stats_df$Cohort, function(lab) {
    col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
    sprintf('<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;"></span>%s',
            col, htmlEscape(lab))
  }, character(1))

  numeric_cols_0idx <- which(!names(stats_df) %in% "Cohort") - 1L
  DT::datatable(
    stats_df, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
    options = list(
      dom = "t", pageLength = 20, scrollX = FALSE, ordering = FALSE,
      columnDefs = list(
        list(targets = numeric_cols_0idx, className = "dt-right"),
        list(targets = 0, width = "200px")
      ),
      rowCallback = DT::JS(
        "function(row, data, dispIndex) {",
        "  if (dispIndex % 2 === 1) { $(row).css('background-color', '#F9FAFB'); }",
        "}"
      ),
      initComplete = DT::JS(
        "function(settings, json) {",
        "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
        "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
        "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
        "    'font-weight':      '600'",
        "  });",
        "}"
      )
    )
  ) |>
    DT::formatRound(columns = c("Median", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max",
                                "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80"), digits = 1)
}

# ── Section 2: Grade Distribution by Cohort ─────────────────────────────
s2_widget <- if (length(cohorts_with_data) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook data available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  tags$div(
    # Fix 1: fixed 3-column grid at 360px rows — see .cvm-histogram-grid
    # rule in the <style> block. No auto-fit / no minmax — overlap-proof.
    class = "cvm-histogram-grid",
    lapply(cohorts_with_data, function(lab) {
      r <- all_data[[lab]]
      col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-filterable cvm-histogram-card",
        `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
        style = "background:#fff;border:1px solid #E0E4EC;border-radius:12px;padding:16px;position:relative;height:360px;overflow:hidden;",
        tags$div(
          style = "display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;margin-bottom:8px;",
          tags$span(style = sprintf(
            "display:inline-block;width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;",
            col)),
          tags$span(style = sprintf(
            "font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:%s;", col),
            lab)
        ),
        ui_plot_box(
          plotly::ggplotly(
            distribution_plot(r$grades_df,
                              title = sprintf("Distribution — %s", lab),
                              cohort_color = col),
            height = 320
          ) |> plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE, responsive = TRUE),
          height = 340
        )
      )
    })
  )
}

# ── Section 3: Students Below Grade Thresholds ──────────────────────────
s3_widget <- ui_plot_box(
  thresholds_plotly_multi(cohort_thresholds, cohort_colors, height = 420),
  height = 440
)

# ── Section 4: Assessment Breakdown by Cohort ───────────────────────────
s4_widget <- if (nrow(cohort_breakdown_df) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook breakdown available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  per_cohort <- cohort_breakdown_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$assessment, .data$cohort) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      median = stats::median(.data$median, na.rm = TRUE),
      n      = sum(.data$n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  per_cohort$display <- ifelse(
    is.na(per_cohort$median) | !is.finite(per_cohort$median),
    NA_character_,
    sprintf("%.1f (%d)", round(per_cohort$median, 1), as.integer(per_cohort$n))
  )
  wide <- per_cohort |>
    dplyr::select("assessment", "cohort", "display") |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "cohort", values_from = "display")
  wide$Type <- assessment_type_from_name(wide$assessment)
  cohort_cols <- intersect(names(all_data), names(wide))
  wide <- wide |>
    dplyr::select("Type", "Assessment" = "assessment", all_of(cohort_cols)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$Type, .data$Assessment)

  type_col_idx   <- which(names(wide) == "Type") - 1L
  assess_col_idx <- which(names(wide) == "Assessment") - 1L
  cohort_idx     <- which(names(wide) %in% cohort_cols) - 1L
  js_cohort_idx  <- paste0("[", paste(cohort_idx, collapse = ","), "]")
  rowcb <- DT::JS(paste0(
    "function(row, data, dispIndex) {",
    "  var idx = ", js_cohort_idx, ";",
    "  idx.forEach(function(i){",
    "    var c = $('td:eq('+(i)+')', row);",
    "    var t = (c.text() || '').trim();",
    "    if (t === '' || t === 'NA') {",
    "      c.text('Not offered').css({'color':'#9AA5B1','font-style':'italic','text-align':'center'});",
    "    } else {",
    "      c.css({'text-align':'right','font-variant-numeric':'tabular-nums'});",
    "    }",
    "  });",
    "  if (dispIndex % 2 === 1) { $(row).css('background-color', '#F9FAFB'); }",
    "  $('td:eq('+(", type_col_idx, ")+')', row).css({'color':'#5A6472','font-size':'11px','text-transform':'uppercase','letter-spacing':'0.05em','font-weight':'600'});",
    "}"
  ))

  DT::datatable(
    wide, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = list(
      dom = "Bfrtip", buttons = c("csv"),
      pageLength = 50, scrollX = FALSE, ordering = TRUE,
      columnDefs = list(
        list(targets = type_col_idx,   width = "130px", className = "dt-left"),
        list(targets = assess_col_idx, className = "dt-left"),
        list(targets = cohort_idx,     className = "dt-right", width = "120px")
      ),
      rowCallback = rowcb,
      initComplete = DT::JS(
        "function(settings, json) {",
        "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
        "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
        "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
        "    'font-weight':      '600'",
        "  });",
        "}"
      )
    )
  )
}

# ── Section 5: Cohort Aggregate Statistics ──────────────────────────────
s5_widget <- if (length(all_data) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook data available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  aggregate_rows <- lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
    r <- all_data[[lab]]
    col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
    cohort_cell <- sprintf(
      '<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;"></span>%s',
      col, htmlEscape(lab)
    )
    if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) {
      return(data.frame(
        Cohort = cohort_cell,
        Median = NA_real_, Mean = NA_real_, SD = NA_real_,
        Min = NA_real_, Max = NA_real_, N = 0L,
        `% ≥ 80` = NA_real_, `% ≥ 90` = NA_real_, `% ≥ 95` = NA_real_,
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
    final <- r$grades_df |>
      dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                            "individual_weighted_overall"))
    df <- if (nrow(final) > 0) final else r$grades_df
    g <- df$grade[is.finite(df$grade)]
    data.frame(
      Cohort = cohort_cell,
      Median = round(stats::median(g), 1),
      Mean   = round(mean(g), 1),
      SD     = round(stats::sd(g), 2),
      Min    = round(min(g), 1),
      Max    = round(max(g), 1),
      N      = as.integer(dplyr::n_distinct(df$id)),
      `% ≥ 80` = round(100 * mean(g >= 80), 1),
      `% ≥ 90` = round(100 * mean(g >= 90), 1),
      `% ≥ 95` = round(100 * mean(g >= 95), 1),
      check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
  aggregate_df <- do.call(rbind, aggregate_rows)
  num_cols_0idx <- which(names(aggregate_df) != "Cohort") - 1L
  DT::datatable(
    aggregate_df, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
    options = list(
      dom = "t", pageLength = 20, scrollX = FALSE, ordering = FALSE,
      columnDefs = list(
        list(targets = 0,             className = "dt-left", width = "220px"),
        list(targets = num_cols_0idx, className = "dt-right")
      ),
      initComplete = DT::JS(
        "function(settings, json) {",
        "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
        "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
        "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
        "    'font-weight':      '600'",
        "  });",
        "}"
      ),
      rowCallback = DT::JS(
        "function(row, data, dispIndex) {",
        "  if (dispIndex % 2 === 1) { $(row).css('background-color', '#F9FAFB'); }",
        "}"
      )
    )
  )
}

# ── Compose: stack the five sections vertically ─────────────────────────
# Each section is a white card per spec: width:100%;display:block;clear:both;
# margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;background:#FFFFFF;border-radius:12px;
# box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07). Inline style keeps the spec
# self-evident at the call site; the .cvm-card class mirrors the same
# values and also wires scoped table styling (Fix 4).
card_style <- paste0(
  "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
  "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
  "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
  "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
)

tagList(
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Grade Performance by Cohort",
      "Overall grade statistics for each graduating class — median, spread, and the share meeting pass thresholds."
    ),
    s1_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Grade Distribution by Cohort",
      "Histogram of final grades for each cohort — shape and spread side by side."
    ),
    s2_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Students Below Grade Thresholds",
      "Counts of students finishing below 70%, 80%, and 90%, grouped by cohort."
    ),
    s3_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Assessment Breakdown by Cohort",
      "Median score and N per assessment, pivoted across cohorts to compare item-level performance over time."
    ),
    s4_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Cohort Aggregate Statistics",
      "Full descriptive statistics per cohort, including pass-rate cuts at 80 / 90 / 95%."
    ),
    s5_widget
  )
)
```

# Curriculum Tag Coverage

```{r}
#| title: "Curriculum coverage"
# Full-width vertical stack — one section per tag set. Per spec:
#   1. Section heading card
#   2. Per-cohort coverage rows (colored dot + x/y tags + % + progress bar)
#   3. Grouped bar chart (tag counts per cohort)
#   4. Multi-column heatmap (one column per cohort)
#   5. Uncovered-tag pills with per-cohort dots (solid = covered, hollow = not)
#
# Implementation: ONE regular R chunk returning a nested tagList. No
# output: asis and no print() inside an asis block — widgets go through
# htmltools' knit_print which correctly registers plotly dependencies.

if (length(tag_set_names) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No tag coverage data available for this course.", variant = "info")
} else {

  # -- htmltools-rendered coverage heatmap -----------------------------
  # The key accreditation visual. Pure HTML grid (no plotly) so:
  #   * cells are guaranteed ≥ 36px tall and show the count number directly
  #   * each column fills white → that cohort's assigned color at the
  #     column's own max (so the same count reads differently across
  #     cohorts by color, which is the point — per-column saturation)
  #   * printable/PDF-friendly: no canvas, no JS required to see it
  render_tag_heatmap <- function(cohort_counts, cohort_colors, all_tags) {
    if (length(all_tags) == 0 || length(cohort_counts) == 0) {
      return(tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472;margin:12px 0;",
                    "No data for heatmap."))
    }
    labels <- names(cohort_counts)
    # Column max for normalization (avoid 0 divide).
    col_max <- vapply(labels, function(lab) {
      vs <- cohort_counts[[lab]]
      m <- suppressWarnings(max(as.numeric(vs[names(vs) %in% all_tags]), na.rm = TRUE))
      if (!is.finite(m) || m <= 0) 1 else m
    }, numeric(1))

    header_row <- tags$div(
      style = sprintf(
        "display:grid;grid-template-columns:240px repeat(%d, 1fr);gap:2px;margin-bottom:2px;",
        length(labels)
      ),
      tags$div(style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.05em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;padding:8px 4px;", "Tag"),
      lapply(labels, function(lab) {
        col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
        tags$div(
          style = sprintf("padding:10px 6px;text-align:center;font-size:11px;font-weight:700;color:#FFFFFF;background:%s;border-radius:6px 6px 0 0;letter-spacing:0.03em;", col),
          lab
        )
      })
    )

    body_rows <- lapply(all_tags, function(t) {
      tags$div(
        style = sprintf(
          "display:grid;grid-template-columns:240px repeat(%d, 1fr);gap:2px;margin-bottom:2px;",
          length(labels)
        ),
        tags$div(
          style = "padding:8px 6px;font-size:12px;color:#1A1A2E;min-height:36px;display:flex;align-items:center;border-right:1px solid #E0E4EC;",
          t
        ),
        lapply(labels, function(lab) {
          col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
          vs <- cohort_counts[[lab]]
          val <- if (t %in% names(vs)) as.numeric(vs[[t]]) else 0
          ratio <- if (is.finite(val) && val > 0) val / col_max[[lab]] else 0
          bg <- hex_rgba(col, ratio)
          # Text color flips to white once fill is dark enough.
          txt_col <- if (ratio > 0.55) "#FFFFFF" else "#1A1A2E"
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf(
              "min-height:36px;background:%s;color:%s;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:12px;font-weight:500;border:1px solid #E4E7EC;border-radius:4px;",
              bg, txt_col
            ),
            title = sprintf("%s — %s: %d", t, lab, as.integer(val)),
            if (val > 0) format(as.integer(val)) else "·"
          )
        })
      )
    })

    tags$div(
      style = "margin:8px 0 16px 0;overflow-x:auto;",
      header_row,
      body_rows
    )
  }

  # -- per-section builder ---------------------------------------------
  build_tagset_section <- function(ts) {
    pretty <- pretty_tagset_name(ts)

    cohort_counts <- stats::setNames(
      lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
        r <- all_data[[lab]]
        if (is.null(r$tag_coverage) || length(r$tag_coverage) == 0) return(integer(0))
        merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                          lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
        tagset_counts_vec(merged)
      }),
      names(all_data)
    )

    # Taxonomy-wide totals for the heading subtitle.
    all_tags_any <- unique(unlist(lapply(cohort_counts, names)))
    all_tags_any <- all_tags_any[!is_deprecated_tag(all_tags_any)]
    n_total_tags <- length(all_tags_any)
    n_any_cov <- sum(vapply(all_tags_any, function(t) {
      any(vapply(cohort_counts, function(cc) {
        v <- if (t %in% names(cc)) cc[[t]] else 0; v > 0
      }, logical(1)))
    }, logical(1)))

    coverage_row <- function(lab) {
      col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
      r <- all_data[[lab]]
      merged <- if (!is.null(r$tag_coverage) && length(r$tag_coverage) > 0) {
        dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
      } else tibble::tibble()
      s <- tag_coverage_summary(merged)
      pct_display <- if (is.na(s$pct)) "—" else sprintf("%.1f%%", s$pct)
      pct_w <- if (is.na(s$pct)) 0 else max(0, min(100, as.numeric(s$pct)))
      # CRITICAL: use CSS GRID (not flex). Quarto auto-injects
      # `html-fill-container` onto every htmltools child, which sets
      # `flex: 1 1 auto` and overrides our per-child min-widths — making
      # flex siblings collapse to near-zero. CSS grid with explicit
      # column tracks is immune to that override.
      # Fix 2: per-spec 5-column grid — 20px dot | 180px name | 120px
      # "X / Y tags" | 60px percentage | 1fr progress bar. Gap 12px,
      # 10px vertical padding, 1px bottom border between rows.
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-filterable",
        `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
        style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:20px 180px 120px 60px 1fr;align-items:center;gap:12px;padding:14px 0;border-bottom:1px solid #F0F0F0;",
        # col 1: colored dot (14px dot centered inside 20px track)
        tags$span(style = sprintf(
          "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
        )),
        # col 2: cohort name
        tags$span(style = "font-weight:600;color:#003F6B;white-space:nowrap;", lab),
        # col 3: "X / Y tags"
        tags$span(style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:13px;white-space:nowrap;",
                  sprintf("%d / %d tags", s$covered, s$total)),
        # col 4: percentage (right-aligned inside its 60px cell)
        tags$span(style = sprintf("font-weight:600;color:%s;white-space:nowrap;text-align:right;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;", col),
                  pct_display),
        # col 5: progress bar fills remaining space
        tags$div(
          style = "background:#E8ECF0;height:10px;border-radius:999px;overflow:hidden;",
          tags$div(style = sprintf(
            "background:%s;height:100%%;width:%.1f%%;border-radius:999px;", col, pct_w))
        )
      )
    }

    # Bar chart: larger min height + more margin for readable tag labels.
    bar <- tag_bar_plotly_multi(
      cohort_counts, cohort_colors, title = pretty,
      height = max(500, 80 + 26 * length(all_tags_any))
    )

    # Heatmap — rebuilt as htmltools grid (see render_tag_heatmap above).
    heatmap_ui <- render_tag_heatmap(cohort_counts, cohort_colors, all_tags_any)

    # Uncovered in EVERY cohort = uncovered_universe. The original helper
    # surfaced "uncovered in ANY cohort"; per spec tightened to "Not
    # covered in any cohort" so the panel is clearly a gap list.
    fully_uncovered <- all_tags_any[vapply(all_tags_any, function(t) {
      all(vapply(cohort_counts, function(cc) {
        v <- if (t %in% names(cc)) cc[[t]] else 0; v == 0
      }, logical(1)))
    }, logical(1))]

    uncovered_ui <- if (length(fully_uncovered) == 0) {
      tags$p(
        style = sprintf("color:%s;font-weight:500;margin:12px 0 0 0;", CVM_PALETTE$success),
        "Every tag is covered in at least one cohort."
      )
    } else {
      tagList(
        tags$p(
          style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;margin:16px 0 8px 0;",
          sprintf("Not covered in any cohort (%d)", length(fully_uncovered))
        ),
        tags$div(
          style = "display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:8px;margin-top:4px;",
          lapply(fully_uncovered, function(t) {
            tags$span(
              style = "display:inline-block;padding:6px 12px;border:1px solid #003F6B;border-radius:20px;color:#003F6B;background:#FFFFFF;font-size:12px;font-weight:500;white-space:nowrap;",
              t
            )
          })
        )
      )
    }

    # Fix 2 + Fix 4: the whole tag-set section is ONE white card per spec
    # (12px radius, subtle shadow, 24px padding). Gold 4px accent rule on
    # the left keeps the brand tag-coverage motif. Inside the card: a
    # compact header row (title + tag-count pill), then sub-sections
    # titled with `.cvm-card-title` (navy 16px/600).
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card",
      style = paste0(
        "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
        "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
        "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-left:4px solid #AB8B00;",
        "border-radius:12px;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
      ),
      # Card header: tag-set title + muted subtitle + gold pill.
      tags$div(
        style = "display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:16px;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-bottom:20px;",
        tags$div(
          tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", pretty),
          tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
                 sprintf("%d covered across all cohorts · %d total in taxonomy",
                         n_any_cov, n_total_tags))
        ),
        tags$span(
          style = "display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;background:#FFF5E0;border:1px solid #E8D78A;color:#8A6D00;padding:6px 14px;border-radius:999px;font-size:13px;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:0.02em;",
          tags$strong(style = "color:#AB8B00;", n_total_tags),
          " tags"
        )
      ),
      tags$h3(class = "cvm-card-title", style = "font-size:14px;margin-top:8px;", "Coverage by cohort"),
      lapply(names(all_data), coverage_row),
      tags$h3(class = "cvm-card-title", style = "font-size:14px;margin-top:24px;", "Tag-by-tag counts (grouped bar chart)"),
      ui_plot_box(bar$plot, height = max(520, 100 + 26 * length(all_tags_any))),
      ui_deprecated_note(bar$deprecated_count),
      tags$h3(class = "cvm-card-title", style = "font-size:14px;margin-top:24px;", "Coverage heatmap — counts per cohort"),
      heatmap_ui,
      uncovered_ui
    )
  }

  do.call(tagList, lapply(tag_set_names, build_tagset_section))
}
```

# Cross-Year Comparison

```{r}
#| title: "Cross-year comparison"
# Redesigned as a set of analyses that mean something for curriculum
# evaluation (per spec):
#
#   1. Grade trajectory — per-cohort arrow vs. previous cohort, ± delta
#   2. Delivery consistency score — CV of medians → High / Medium / Low
#   3. Student volume trend — bar chart of N students per cohort
#   4. Top 5 most consistently covered tags (highest minimum count)
#   5. Top 5 most variable tags (curriculum-gap signals)
#   6. Assessment difficulty signal — per-assessment trend across cohorts,
#      decline > 5 points flagged
#
# All analyses come from helpers in R/comparison_engine.R so the same code
# powers the rule-based narrative and this tab.

if (length(all_data) < 2) {
  ui_banner("Only one cohort has data for this course — cross-year comparison requires two or more.", variant = "info")
} else {
  traj         <- compute_cohort_trajectory(all_data)
  consistency  <- compute_consistency_score(all_data)
  volume       <- compute_student_volume(all_data)
  assess_trend <- compute_assessment_trends(all_data, min_cohorts = 3)

  # Re-derive cohort_counts_by_ts for tag stats.
  cohort_counts_by_ts <- stats::setNames(
    lapply(tag_set_names, function(ts) {
      stats::setNames(
        lapply(all_data, function(r) {
          if (is.null(r$tag_coverage) || length(r$tag_coverage) == 0) return(integer(0))
          merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                            lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
          tagset_counts_vec(merged)
        }),
        names(all_data)
      )
    }),
    tag_set_names
  )
  tag_stats <- compute_tag_stats(cohort_counts_by_ts)
  top_c <- top_consistent_tags(tag_stats, 5)
  top_v <- top_variable_tags(tag_stats, 5)

  # ---------- 1. Grade trajectory ----------
  trajectory_ui <- {
    rows <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(traj)), function(i) {
      row <- traj[i, ]
      col <- cohort_color_for(row$cohort)
      trend <- row$trend
      # "anomaly" = this cohort's own median > 100 (extra-credit scale).
      # We surface it explicitly rather than producing a nonsense delta.
      arrow <- switch(trend %||% "NA",
                      up      = "▲",
                      down    = "▼",
                      stable  = "▬",
                      anomaly = "⚠",
                      "—")
      arrow_color <- switch(trend %||% "NA",
                            up      = CVM_PALETTE$success,
                            down    = CVM_PALETTE$warning,
                            stable  = CVM_PALETTE$muted,
                            anomaly = CVM_PALETTE$alert,
                            CVM_PALETTE$muted)
      delta_text <- if (identical(trend, "anomaly")) {
        "Scale anomaly — median > 100% (extra-credit-inflated)"
      } else if (is.na(row$delta)) {
        "(no valid prior cohort)"
      } else {
        sprintf("%+.1f vs %s",
                row$delta,
                sub("^Class of ", "GC",
                    substr(row$prev_cohort,
                           nchar(row$prev_cohort) - 3,
                           nchar(row$prev_cohort))))
      }
      # Trajectory rows live inside a white .cvm-card, so the row itself
      # is transparent with a subtle bottom border — matches tag-coverage
      # rows visually.
      tags$div(
        style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:20px 180px 40px 140px 1fr;align-items:center;gap:12px;padding:14px 0;border-bottom:1px solid #F0F0F0;",
        tags$span(style = sprintf(
          "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
        )),
        tags$span(style = "font-weight:600;color:#003F6B;white-space:nowrap;", row$cohort),
        tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:22px;font-weight:700;color:%s;text-align:center;", arrow_color), arrow),
        tags$span(style = "color:#1A1A2E;white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
                  sprintf("Median %.1f", row$median)),
        tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:12px;color:%s;", arrow_color), delta_text)
      )
    })
    rows
  }

  # ---------- 2. Consistency score ----------
  cv_display <- if (is.null(consistency$cv) || is.na(consistency$cv)) "—"
                else sprintf("%.2f%%", consistency$cv)
  consistency_bar <- switch(consistency$label,
                            High   = CVM_PALETTE$success,
                            Medium = CVM_PALETTE$warning,
                            Low    = CVM_PALETTE$alert,
                            CVM_PALETTE$muted)
  # Hero pull-quote rendered flush inside the Trajectory card's right
  # column. Left accent rule preserves the status color (success / warn
  # / alert) without reintroducing a nested-card shadow.
  consistency_ui <- tags$div(
    style = sprintf(
      "border-left:4px solid %s;padding:8px 0 8px 20px;min-height:200px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;",
      consistency_bar
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = "font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;margin-bottom:12px;",
      "Delivery consistency"
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = sprintf("font-size:56px;font-weight:700;color:%s;line-height:1;letter-spacing:-0.02em;", consistency_bar),
      consistency$label
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = "font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;margin-top:10px;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
      sprintf("CV = %s across %d cohorts", cv_display, length(all_data))
    ),
    tags$p(
      style = "margin:16px 0 0 0;font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;line-height:1.5;",
      "Low CV = stable course delivery year over year. High CV flags year-to-year inconsistency that warrants investigation."
    )
  )

  # ---------- 3. Student volume ----------
  # Data labels on top of each bar per spec.
  volume_bar <- plotly::plot_ly(
    data = volume,
    x = ~cohort, y = ~students, type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = unname(cohort_colors[volume$cohort])),
    text = ~students,
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont = list(color = "#1A1A2E", size = 12, family = "Inter"),
    hovertemplate = "%{x}<br>%{y} students<extra></extra>",
    height = 320
  ) |>
    plotly::layout(
      title = list(text = "Students assessed per cohort",
                   font = list(color = CVM_PALETTE$navy, size = 14)),
      xaxis = list(title = "", tickangle = -20, type = "category", automargin = TRUE),
      yaxis = list(title = "N students", gridcolor = "#E4E7EC",
                   rangemode = "tozero",
                   # Headroom for the data label on the tallest bar.
                   range = c(0, max(volume$students, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.15)),
      paper_bgcolor = "white", plot_bgcolor = "white",
      font = list(color = CVM_PALETTE$text, family = "Inter, sans-serif"),
      margin = list(t = 50, b = 80, l = 60, r = 40),
      showlegend = FALSE, autosize = TRUE
    ) |>
    plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE, responsive = TRUE)

  # ---------- 4 + 5. Top consistent / variable tags ----------
  # Inner mini-cards — soft background + accent rule; sit inside the
  # parent .cvm-card so they intentionally have no drop shadow.
  tag_card <- function(title, df, metric_col, metric_label, accent) {
    tags$div(
      style = sprintf(
        "background:#F9FAFB;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;",
        accent
      ),
      tags$div(
        style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;margin-bottom:10px;",
        title
      ),
      if (nrow(df) == 0) {
        tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:13px;margin:0;",
               "Not enough tag variance across cohorts to rank.")
      } else {
        tags$ol(
          style = "margin:0;padding-left:20px;",
          lapply(seq_len(nrow(df)), function(i) {
            row <- df[i, ]
            tags$li(
              style = "padding:4px 0;font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;",
              tags$strong(row$tag),
              tags$span(
                style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:11px;margin-left:6px;",
                sprintf("(%s · %s %s)",
                        pretty_tagset_name(row$tagset),
                        metric_label,
                        if (is.numeric(row[[metric_col]])) sprintf("%.1f", row[[metric_col]])
                        else as.character(row[[metric_col]]))
              )
            )
          })
        )
      }
    )
  }
  # Fix 3: explicit two-column CSS grid (not auto-fit / not floated / not
  # absolutely positioned) so the two tag-signal cards sit side-by-side
  # without overlap.
  tag_cards_ui <- tags$div(
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:24px;margin-top:8px;",
    tag_card("Top 5 most consistently covered tags",
             top_c, "min", "min count",
             CVM_PALETTE$success),
    tag_card("Top 5 most variable tags (possible gaps)",
             top_v, "sd", "SD",
             CVM_PALETTE$warning)
  )

  # ---------- 6. Assessment difficulty signal ----------
  assess_ui <- if (nrow(assess_trend) == 0) {
    ui_banner(paste("No assessment has data in 3+ cohorts yet — difficulty",
                    "trends will become available as more cohorts finish."),
              variant = "info")
  } else {
    df <- assess_trend
    df$Flag <- vapply(df$flag, function(f) {
      switch(f,
             declining = "⚠ declining",
             improving = "↑ improving",
             stable    = "= stable",
             f)
    }, character(1))
    dt <- DT::datatable(
      df |>
        dplyr::transmute(
          Assessment  = .data$assessment,
          `Cohorts`   = .data$n_cohorts,
          `First mean`= .data$first_mean,
          `Last mean` = .data$last_mean,
          Δ           = .data$delta,
          Flag        = .data$Flag
        ),
      rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
      options  = list(
        dom = "tp", pageLength = 15, scrollX = FALSE,
        columnDefs = list(
          list(targets = 0, className = "dt-left"),
          list(targets = 1:5, className = "dt-right")
        )
      )
    ) |>
      DT::formatStyle(
        "Flag",
        backgroundColor = DT::styleEqual(
          c("⚠ declining", "↑ improving", "= stable"),
          c("#FFEBEE", "#E8F5E9", "#F5F6FA"))
      )
    n_declining <- sum(df$flag == "declining")
    tagList(
      if (n_declining > 0) {
        ui_banner(sprintf(
          "%d assessment%s showing a mean decline of more than 5 points from earliest to latest cohort — investigate.",
          n_declining, if (n_declining == 1) "" else "s"
        ), variant = "warn")
      },
      dt
    )
  }

  # ---------- Assemble ----------
  # Fix 3 + Fix 4: every cross-year section is its own white .cvm-card
  # with 12px radius, subtle shadow, 24px padding. Titles use the navy
  # 16px/600 `.cvm-card-title`. Cards clear floats and block-level so
  # the four panels stack cleanly, zero overlap.
  cy_card_style <- paste0(
    "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
    "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
    "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
    "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
  )
  tagList(
    # Card 1: Grade trajectory + Delivery-consistency pull-quote side by side.
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Grade trajectory — cohort over cohort"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Each cohort's median compared to the prior cohort's median, with a delivery-consistency verdict derived from the CV."),
      tags$div(
        style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:2fr 1fr;gap:24px;align-items:start;",
        tags$div(trajectory_ui),
        tags$div(consistency_ui)
      )
    ),
    # Card 2: Student volume trend.
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Student volume trend"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Number of students assessed in each cohort — tracks enrollment and curriculum reach."),
      ui_plot_box(volume_bar, height = 380)
    ),
    # Card 3: Tag-coverage signals (two-column grid).
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Tag-coverage signals"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Which tags are reliably covered every year vs. which swing cohort to cohort (potential curriculum gaps)."),
      tag_cards_ui
    ),
    # Card 4: Assessment difficulty signal.
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Assessment difficulty signal"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Per-assessment mean trend across cohorts — declines > 5 points are flagged for faculty review."),
      assess_ui
    )
  )
}
```

# Course Insights

```{r}
narr_path <- sub("\\.rds$", "_narrative.rds", params$all_data_path)
narrative <- if (file.exists(narr_path)) readRDS(narr_path) else NULL

if (is.null(narrative)) {
  ui_banner("Course narrative not generated for this render.", variant = "info")
} else if (isFALSE(narrative$available %||% TRUE)) {
  HTML(paste0(
    '<div class="cvm-narrative"><h3>Course narrative not available</h3><p>',
    htmlEscape(narrative$narrative),
    '</p></div>'
  ))
} else {
  # Flag cards — icon + colored left border + title + description.
  sev_palette <- list(
    high   = list(bar = "#C62828", bg = "#FFEBEE", text = "#8E1A1A", icon = "!", label = "HIGH PRIORITY"),
    medium = list(bar = "#F57C00", bg = "#FFF3E0", text = "#8A4A00", icon = "!", label = "MEDIUM PRIORITY"),
    low    = list(bar = "#2E7D32", bg = "#E8F5E9", text = "#1B5E20", icon = "✓", label = "LOW PRIORITY")
  )
  flags_ui <- if (!is.null(narrative$flags) && nrow(narrative$flags) > 0) {
    tagList(lapply(seq_len(nrow(narrative$flags)), function(i) {
      f <- narrative$flags[i, ]
      p <- sev_palette[[f$severity]] %||% sev_palette$medium
      tags$div(
        style = sprintf(
          "display:grid;grid-template-columns:32px 1fr;column-gap:14px;align-items:start;padding:16px 20px;margin-bottom:12px;background:%s;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:8px;",
          p$bg, p$bar
        ),
        # Circular icon badge
        tags$span(
          style = sprintf(
            "width:28px;height:28px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;color:#FFFFFF;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-weight:700;font-size:14px;",
            p$bar
          ),
          p$icon
        ),
        tags$div(
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf("font-size:10px;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:0.1em;color:%s;margin-bottom:4px;", p$bar),
            p$label
          ),
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf("font-size:14px;font-weight:600;color:%s;margin-bottom:6px;line-height:1.4;", p$text),
            f$issue
          ),
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf("font-size:13px;color:%s;line-height:1.5;opacity:0.85;", p$text),
            f$recommendation
          )
        )
      )
    }))
  } else NULL

  # Questions — numbered cards. CSS grid for the inner layout so Quarto's
  # html-fill-container can't collapse the question text to 0 width.
  questions_ui <- if (length(narrative$questions) > 0) {
    tags$div(
      style = "display:grid;row-gap:10px;",
      lapply(seq_along(narrative$questions), function(i) {
        q <- narrative$questions[[i]]
        tags$div(
          style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:32px 1fr;column-gap:14px;align-items:start;padding:14px 18px;background:#EEF4FB;border-left:3px solid #003F6B;border-radius:8px;",
          tags$span(
            style = "width:28px;height:28px;border-radius:50%;background:#003F6B;color:#FFFFFF;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-weight:700;font-size:13px;",
            paste0("Q", i)
          ),
          tags$div(
            style = "font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;line-height:1.55;word-break:normal;",
            q
          )
        )
      })
    )
  } else NULL

  # Distinguish rule-based vs LLM — the former gets a "Generated from data"
  # badge; the latter keeps the model attribution.
  source_kind <- narrative$source %||% "llm"
  badge <- if (source_kind == "rule-based") {
    tags$span(
      style = "display:inline-block;padding:4px 10px;background:#EEF4FB;color:#003F6B;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;border-radius:999px;border:1px solid #CDE0ED;",
      "Generated from data"
    )
  } else {
    tags$span(
      style = "display:inline-block;padding:4px 10px;background:#FFF5E0;color:#8A6D00;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;border-radius:999px;border:1px solid #E8D78A;",
      "Model-generated"
    )
  }
  footer_line <- if (source_kind == "rule-based") {
    "Narrative is computed from the data. It does not substitute for faculty review."
  } else {
    sprintf("Generated %s using %s.",
            fmt_ts(narrative$generated_at),
            narrative$model %||% "—")
  }

  # Fix 4: each insight block is a white card — narrative, flags, and
  # questions each live in their own .cvm-card. Footer attribution sits
  # below the last card as muted microcopy.
  ci_card_style <- paste0(
    "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
    "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
    "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
    "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
  )
  tagList(
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card cvm-narrative",
      style = ci_card_style,
      tags$div(
        style = "display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;margin-bottom:12px;",
        badge
      ),
      HTML(markdown_to_html(narrative$narrative))
    ),
    if (!is.null(flags_ui)) {
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-card", style = ci_card_style,
        tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Areas of concern"),
        tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
               "Issues ranked by severity with specific faculty follow-up recommendations."),
        flags_ui
      )
    },
    if (!is.null(questions_ui)) {
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-card", style = ci_card_style,
        tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Questions to explore"),
        tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
               "Prompts to carry into the next faculty curriculum-review conversation."),
        questions_ui
      )
    },
    tags$p(
      style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:12px;margin:-24px 0 32px 0;padding:0 4px;",
      footer_line
    )
  )
}
```

# Downloads

```{r}
# Filter-aware Downloads tab.
#
# - Export Artifacts: ONE compact card listing PPTX + Excel if present;
#   a single-line info banner if not.
# - Per-cohort Survey cards: each wrapped in .cvm-filterable so the chip
#   filter bar shows only cards for currently selected cohorts. Cohort
#   label as a colored card header; PDF rows compact inside.
# - Compact banners (not full-height) for empty states.
#
# params$all_data_path points at .render_cache/all_years_result.rds;
# the course folder (where PPTX/XLSX land) is two levels up.
course_dir <- dirname(dirname(params$all_data_path))

pptx_files <- list.files(course_dir, pattern = "\\.pptx$",          full.names = TRUE)
xlsx_files <- list.files(course_dir, pattern = "_workbooks\\.zip$", full.names = TRUE)

has_exports <- length(pptx_files) > 0 || length(xlsx_files) > 0

dl_card_style <- paste0(
  "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
  "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
  "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
  "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
)

exports_card <- tags$div(
  class = "cvm-card",
  style = dl_card_style,
  tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Export artifacts"),
  tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
         "Generated PowerPoint and Excel workbooks for this course portfolio."),
  if (has_exports) {
    tags$div(
      style = "display:grid;gap:10px;",
      if (length(pptx_files) > 0) {
        ui_download_row("PowerPoint", basename(pptx_files[[1]]),
                        basename(pptx_files[[1]]),
                        file.info(pptx_files[[1]])$size)
      },
      if (length(xlsx_files) > 0) {
        ui_download_row("Excel workbooks (zip)", basename(xlsx_files[[1]]),
                        basename(xlsx_files[[1]]),
                        file.info(xlsx_files[[1]])$size)
      }
    )
  } else {
    tags$p(
      style = "margin:0;font-size:13px;color:#5A6472;",
      HTML("No PowerPoint or Excel workbook yet. Re-run with <code>--with-exports</code> to generate them.")
    )
  }
)

# Per-cohort survey cards — one .cvm-filterable card per cohort so the
# chip filter bar shows only the active cohorts.
survey_cards <- lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
  s_dir_abs <- surveys_dir(r$course_code, r$cohort_label)
  safe_code <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "", r$course_code)
  class_slug_local <- cohort_slug(r$cohort_label)
  rel_base <- file.path("..", sprintf("%s_%s", safe_code, class_slug_local), "surveys")
  drop_path_rel <- sub("^.*/outputs/", "outputs/", s_dir_abs)

  # Card header — colored dot + cohort name in cohort color.
  card_header <- tags$div(
    style = sprintf("display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;padding-bottom:12px;margin-bottom:14px;border-bottom:1px solid #E0E4EC;"),
    tags$span(style = sprintf(
      "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
    )),
    tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:16px;font-weight:700;color:%s;", col), lab)
  )

  body <- if (!dir.exists(s_dir_abs)) {
    ready_on <- if (!is.null(r$max_period_finish_date) && !is.na(r$max_period_finish_date)) {
      format(as.Date(r$max_period_finish_date) + 2)
    } else "(period end date unknown)"
    tags$p(
      style = "margin:0;font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;",
      sprintf("Survey folder will be created 2 days after course end (%s).",
              ready_on)
    )
  } else {
    pdfs <- list.files(s_dir_abs, pattern = "\\.pdf$", full.names = TRUE)
    if (length(pdfs) == 0) {
      tags$p(
        style = "margin:0;font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;",
        HTML(sprintf(
          "Survey folder ready — drop PDF files at <code>%s</code>",
          htmlEscape(drop_path_rel)
        ))
      )
    } else {
      # Fix 3: the "To add more, drop PDFs at …" microcopy was removed
      # per spec. PDF rows only.
      tags$div(
        style = "display:grid;gap:8px;",
        lapply(pdfs, function(p) {
          ui_download_row(
            label      = "Survey PDF",
            href       = file.path(rel_base, basename(p)),
            filename   = basename(p),
            size_bytes = file.info(p)$size
          )
        })
      )
    }
  }

  # Fix 5: force survey cards visible by default. The .cvm-filterable
  # class is only hidden by the filter JS when a chip is deselected — on
  # initial load every card must render. `display:block !important`
  # overrides any stray Quarto / bootstrap rule that would hide the card
  # before JS boots.
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-filterable",
    `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
    style = sprintf(
      "display:block !important;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #E0E4EC;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:12px;padding:20px 24px;box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);",
      col
    ),
    card_header, body
  )
})

# Surveys section: one outer .cvm-card containing a responsive grid of
# per-cohort sub-cards (each still .cvm-filterable). The outer card gives
# the section a navy 16px title and consistent chrome; the inner cards
# carry the cohort-colored left border.
surveys_card <- tags$div(
  class = "cvm-card",
  style = dl_card_style,
  tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Course surveys by cohort"),
  tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
         "End-of-course survey PDFs available for each graduating class."),
  tags$div(
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(420px,1fr));gap:20px;",
    survey_cards
  )
)

tagList(exports_card, surveys_card)
```

```{r}
#| include: false
#| output: false
# Filter-bar JSON payload — next chunk writes the raw HTML block.
```

```{=html}
<script id="cvm-dashboard-data" type="application/json">
`r period_index_json`
</script>
<script id="cvm-cohort-colors" type="application/json">
`r cohort_colors_json`
</script>
<script id="cvm-course-code" type="application/json">
"`r primary$course_code`"
</script>
<style>
  /* Sticky filter bar — scoped via .cvm-* classes so nothing collides with
     Quarto's Bootstrap layout. */
  .cvm-filter-bar {
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 100;
    background: #FFFFFF;
    box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
    padding: 12px 24px;
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; flex-wrap: wrap;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #E0E4EC;
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
  }
  .cvm-filter-bar .label {
    font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600;
    text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em;
    color: #5A6472;
  }
  .cvm-filter-bar .chips { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; }
  .cvm-chip {
    display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
    padding: 6px 14px;
    border: 2px solid transparent;
    border-radius: 20px;
    background: #E8ECF0;
    color: #9AA5B1;
    font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer; user-select: none;
    transition: background 120ms, color 120ms, border-color 120ms;
  }
  .cvm-chip .chip-dot {
    display: inline-block;
    width: 10px; height: 10px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    background: transparent;
    border: 2px solid #9AA5B1;
  }
  .cvm-chip:hover { background: #F0F4F8; }
  .cvm-chip.active {
    background: #FFFFFF;
    color: var(--chip-color, #003F6B);
    border-color: var(--chip-color, #003F6B);
  }
  .cvm-chip.active .chip-dot {
    background: var(--chip-color, #003F6B);
    border-color: var(--chip-color, #003F6B);
  }
  .cvm-chip:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid #AB8B00;
    outline-offset: 2px;
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions { margin-left: auto; display: flex; gap: 14px; }
  .cvm-filter-actions a {
    color: #AB8B00; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer; text-decoration: none;
    border-bottom: 1px dashed rgba(171,139,0,0.4);
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions a:hover { color: #003F6B; border-color: #003F6B; }
  .cvm-filter-actions a:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid #AB8B00; outline-offset: 2px;
  }
  .cvm-filterable { transition: opacity 150ms; }

  /* --- Fix 1: histogram grid — fixed 3-column layout, every card exactly
     360px tall, nothing absolutely-positioned so cards cannot overlap. */
  .cvm-histogram-grid {
    display: grid !important;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr) !important;
    grid-auto-rows: 360px !important;
    gap: 16px !important;
  }
  .cvm-histogram-grid > .cvm-histogram-card {
    position: relative !important;
    height: 360px !important;
    margin: 0 !important;
  }

  /* --- Fix 4: Course Insights narrative — never clip; paragraphs breathe. */
  .cvm-narrative {
    overflow-y: visible !important;
    height: auto !important;
    max-height: none !important;
  }
  .cvm-narrative p { margin-bottom: 16px; }

  /* --- Design pass (Brand Guardian / UI Designer / UX Architect):
     Single card token reused across every tab's content sections.
     White background, 12px radius, subtle shadow, 24px padding,
     full-width block that clears floats so nothing overlaps. */
  .cvm-card {
    width: 100% !important;
    display: block !important;
    clear: both !important;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    background: #FFFFFF;
    border: 1px solid #EEF1F5;
    border-radius: 12px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);
    padding: 24px;
    margin-bottom: 48px;
  }
  .cvm-card > :first-child { margin-top: 0; }
  .cvm-card > :last-child  { margin-bottom: 0; }

  /* Section title + description inside a card. Navy 16px/600 per spec. */
  .cvm-card-title {
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
    font-size: 16px;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #003F6B;
    margin: 0 0 6px 0;
    letter-spacing: -0.005em;
  }
  .cvm-card-desc {
    font-size: 13px;
    color: #5A6472;
    margin: 0 0 20px 0;
    line-height: 1.5;
  }

  /* --- Numeric tables (Visual Storyteller / UI Designer):
     Navy header, alternating #F9FAFB row shading, right-aligned numbers,
     consistent padding. Scoped under `.cvm-card` so it never touches
     Overview. */
  .cvm-card table.dataTable thead th {
    background-color: #003F6B !important;
    color: #FFFFFF !important;
    font-weight: 600 !important;
    border-bottom: 0 !important;
    letter-spacing: 0.01em;
  }
  .cvm-card table.dataTable tbody tr:nth-child(even) > td {
    background-color: #F9FAFB !important;
  }
  .cvm-card table.dataTable tbody td { border-top: 1px solid #F0F2F5; padding: 10px 12px; }
  .cvm-card table.dataTable td.dt-right,
  .cvm-card table.dataTable th.dt-right { text-align: right; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

  /* --- Charts (Visual Storyteller): 380px floor, 16px internal padding,
     no clipping. Applies to plotly + htmlwidget containers inside cards. */
  .cvm-card .plotly.html-widget,
  .cvm-card .html-widget,
  .cvm-chart {
    min-height: 380px !important;
    padding: 16px;
    box-sizing: border-box;
  }

  /* --- Tab-pane hygiene: each redesigned tab scrolls vertically on long
     content so cards never clip under the navbar. Overview is explicitly
     excluded — per directive, do not touch it. */
  .tab-pane#assessment-performance,
  .tab-pane#curriculum-tag-coverage,
  .tab-pane#cross-year-comparison,
  .tab-pane#course-insights,
  .tab-pane#downloads {
    overflow-y: auto !important;
    height: auto !important;
  }
</style>
<script>
(function() {
  "use strict";

  // ---- payload ----------------------------------------------------------
  function parseScript(id) {
    const el = document.getElementById(id);
    if (!el) return null;
    try { return JSON.parse(el.textContent); }
    catch (e) { console.error("cvm filter: could not parse", id, e); return null; }
  }
  const PERIODS = parseScript("cvm-dashboard-data") || [];
  const COHORT_COLORS = parseScript("cvm-cohort-colors") || {};
  const COURSE_CODE = parseScript("cvm-course-code") || "default";

  // PERIODS may be per-period (one entry per cperiod_id). Roll up to cohorts.
  // Each cohort is the filter unit; each period knows its cohort.
  const COHORTS = [];
  const seen = new Set();
  PERIODS.forEach(function(p) {
    if (!p || !p.cohort_label) return;
    if (seen.has(p.cohort_label)) return;
    seen.add(p.cohort_label);
    COHORTS.push({
      label: p.cohort_label,
      slug:  p.cohort_slug,
      chip:  (p.chip_label || p.label || p.cohort_label),
      color: COHORT_COLORS[p.cohort_label] || "#003F6B"
    });
  });
  // Ensure consistent chronological order (alphabetic on Class of YYYY).
  COHORTS.sort(function(a, b) { return a.label < b.label ? -1 : 1; });

  if (COHORTS.length === 0) {
    console.warn("cvm filter: no cohorts — filter bar not initialized");
    return;
  }

  // ---- state ------------------------------------------------------------
  const STORAGE_KEY = "cvm-filter-" + COURSE_CODE;
  const ALL_LABELS = COHORTS.map(function(c) { return c.label; });
  let active;
  try {
    const saved = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY) || "null");
    active = Array.isArray(saved)
      ? new Set(saved.filter(function(l) { return ALL_LABELS.indexOf(l) >= 0; }))
      : new Set(ALL_LABELS);
    if (active.size === 0) active = new Set(ALL_LABELS);
  } catch (e) { active = new Set(ALL_LABELS); }

  function persist() {
    try { localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(Array.from(active))); }
    catch (e) { /* private mode — ignore */ }
  }

  // ---- helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
  function median(xs) {
    if (xs.length === 0) return null;
    const s = xs.slice().sort(function(a, b) { return a - b; });
    const m = Math.floor(s.length / 2);
    return s.length % 2 ? s[m] : (s[m - 1] + s[m]) / 2;
  }

  // selectedPeriods = per-period rows whose cohort is active.
  function selectedPeriods() {
    return PERIODS.filter(function(p) { return active.has(p.cohort_label); });
  }

  function recomputeKpis() {
    const sel = selectedPeriods();
    const medians = sel.map(function(p) { return p.median; })
                       .filter(function(v) { return typeof v === "number"; });
    const students = sel.reduce(function(s, p) { return s + (p.students || 0); }, 0);
    const periods  = sel.length;
    const tagSets  = sel.reduce(function(m, p) {
      return Math.max(m, p.n_tag_sets || 0);
    }, 0);

    setKpi("median",   medians.length ? median(medians).toFixed(1) : "—");
    setKpi("students", students);
    setKpi("periods",  periods);
    setKpi("tag_sets", tagSets);
  }
  function setKpi(key, val) {
    document.querySelectorAll('[data-kpi="' + key + '"] .cvm-kpi-value').forEach(function(el) {
      el.textContent = String(val);
    });
  }

  function apply() {
    // Chip visual state
    document.querySelectorAll(".cvm-chip").forEach(function(chip) {
      const on = active.has(chip.dataset.cohortLabel);
      chip.classList.toggle("active", on);
      chip.setAttribute("aria-pressed", on ? "true" : "false");
    });
    // Filterable content blocks — data-cohort-labels is a comma-separated
    // list of cohort labels. Cohort labels contain spaces (e.g. "Class of
    // 2023") so we MUST split only on commas. Splitting on whitespace
    // shreds labels into ["Class","of","2023"] and hides every block.
    document.querySelectorAll(".cvm-filterable").forEach(function(el) {
      const raw = el.dataset.cohortLabels || "";
      const labels = raw.split(",").map(function(s) { return s.trim(); }).filter(Boolean);
      const match = labels.length === 0 ? true : labels.some(function(l) { return active.has(l); });
      // Use setProperty with "important" so we can beat any inline
      // `display:... !important` (e.g. Fix 5 survey cards). Removing the
      // property on match lets the element fall back to its natural
      // `display` — whether that's block, grid-item, flex-item, etc.
      if (match) {
        el.style.removeProperty("display");
      } else {
        el.style.setProperty("display", "none", "important");
      }
    });
    recomputeKpis();
  }

  // ---- build the bar ----------------------------------------------------
  function build() {
    const nav = document.querySelector(".quarto-dashboard .navbar")
             || document.querySelector("header.navbar")
             || document.querySelector("nav");
    if (!nav) { console.warn("cvm filter: no navbar anchor found"); return; }
    if (document.querySelector(".cvm-filter-bar")) return;  // already built

    const bar = document.createElement("div");
    bar.className = "cvm-filter-bar";
    bar.setAttribute("role", "toolbar");
    bar.setAttribute("aria-label", "Filter by graduating class");

    const label = document.createElement("div");
    label.className = "label";
    label.textContent = "Cohorts";
    bar.appendChild(label);

    const chipsWrap = document.createElement("div");
    chipsWrap.className = "chips";
    COHORTS.forEach(function(c) {
      const chip = document.createElement("button");
      chip.type = "button";
      chip.className = "cvm-chip";
      chip.dataset.cohortLabel = c.label;
      chip.style.setProperty("--chip-color", c.color);
      chip.setAttribute("aria-pressed", "false");
      chip.setAttribute("aria-label", "Toggle " + c.label);
      const dot = document.createElement("span");
      dot.className = "chip-dot";
      chip.appendChild(dot);
      const txt = document.createElement("span");
      txt.textContent = c.chip;
      chip.appendChild(txt);
      chip.addEventListener("click", function() {
        if (active.has(c.label)) active.delete(c.label);
        else active.add(c.label);
        if (active.size === 0) active = new Set(ALL_LABELS);
        persist(); apply();
      });
      chipsWrap.appendChild(chip);
    });
    bar.appendChild(chipsWrap);

    const actions = document.createElement("div");
    actions.className = "cvm-filter-actions";
    const selAll = document.createElement("a");
    selAll.textContent = "Select all";
    selAll.setAttribute("role", "button");
    selAll.setAttribute("tabindex", "0");
    selAll.addEventListener("click", function() { active = new Set(ALL_LABELS); persist(); apply(); });
    const clear = document.createElement("a");
    clear.textContent = "Clear";
    clear.setAttribute("role", "button");
    clear.setAttribute("tabindex", "0");
    clear.addEventListener("click", function() {
      active = new Set();
      persist(); apply();
      // 3s safety — reset to all if still empty.
      setTimeout(function() {
        if (active.size === 0) { active = new Set(ALL_LABELS); persist(); apply(); }
      }, 3000);
    });
    actions.appendChild(selAll); actions.appendChild(clear);
    bar.appendChild(actions);

    nav.parentNode.insertBefore(bar, nav.nextSibling);
  }

  // ---- init -------------------------------------------------------------
  let booted = false;
  function runOnce() {
    if (!booted) {
      booted = true;
      try { build(); } catch (e) { console.error("cvm filter: build failed", e); }
    }
    try {
      apply();
      console.log("cvm filter: apply OK, " + COHORTS.length + " cohorts, active=" + active.size);
    } catch (e) { console.error("cvm filter: apply failed", e); }
  }
  if (document.readyState === "loading") {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", runOnce);
  } else {
    runOnce();
  }
  window.addEventListener("load", runOnce);
  setTimeout(runOnce, 300);
})();
</script>
```
